$comment: Prompt Goblin brand source-of-truth for the ad engine. Every value here is EXTRACTED from the live site — styles.css (palette, fonts), app.jsx (voice, taglines, tiers, prices, positioning), goblin-logo.jsx + sprites.jsx (the mark). Do not invent values; if the site changes, re-extract. This doubles as the brand-guidelines.md payload for Claude Design and the brand-conditioning block for image models.

brand.name: Prompt Goblin
brand.oneLiner: AI search visibility & technical SEO — plus accessibility — from a one-goblin shop.
brand.positioning: A solo, honest-broker shop for AI-search visibility (AEO/GEO), technical SEO, and WCAG 2.1 AA / Section 508 accessibility. Gov-forward ICP (accessibility is legally mandated for government); commercial kept. Measures who answer engines actually cite, then ships human-reviewed schema/crawl/content/a11y fixes. Days, not quarters.
brand.archetype: The Trickster-Craftsman — an irreverent goblin who does rigorous, accountable work. Outsider energy, insider competence.

palette.$note: Hex/RGBA pulled verbatim from styles.css :root and the body[data-palette] themes. 'dark' is the default brand skin; 'bone' is the light theme; 'noir' and 'rust' are alternate skins. Use dark+lime as the primary ad look unless a campaign calls for light.

bone.label: Bone (light)
bone.$note: On the light theme the lime DARKENS to #4d7c0f / #3f6212 to keep AA contrast. Never put #A3E635 text on a light background.

rules: Lime #A3E635 is the single hero accent on dark — one screaming color, used sparingly: the CTA, one keyword, the goblin glow.
Body and headlines on dark are bone #E9E7DC, never pure white.
On the bone/light theme, demote lime to #4d7c0f for any text or fine lines (AA contrast).
fire/ice/bolt are SPELL accents (AEO=fire, A11Y=ice, SEO=bolt) — use only for the three-discipline motif, not as general brand color.
Backgrounds carry a faint scanline texture and ~5% film grain overlay.

typography.$note: Four faces pulled from styles.css custom props (--pixel, --pixel-soft, --pixel-tiny, --mono) and confirmed in the colophon ('Set in Press Start 2P · VT323 · JetBrains Mono').

display.role: Headlines, hero, prices, big numbers. Chunky 8-bit pixel face. Tight leading, set BIG, sentence case (not all-caps) for hero lines.

numerals.role: Marquee, large quote text, big secondary numerals. Softer pixel terminal face.

labels.role: Tiny UPPERCASE labels, kickers, button text, tags. letter-spacing ~0.06em, text-transform uppercase, ~9–12px.

body.role: All body copy, terminal lines, form fields. The workhorse monospace. font-feature-settings calt, ss01.

rules: Everything is monospace or pixel — there is NO proportional sans/serif anywhere. This is load-bearing to the brand.
Hero headlines: Press Start 2P, sentence case, one keyword in lime.
Body: JetBrains Mono, sentence case, comfortable measure (~30–56ch).

logo.name: Goblin head mark
logo.description: A line-art goblin head: a wide horned/eared crown with two upright ears, two glowing eyes, a fanged grin, framed by mirrored bracket strokes. Rendered as a single-color vector that inherits currentColor — lime #A3E635 on dark, with a double drop-shadow lime glow in the hero.
logo.vectorSource: goblin-logo.jsx (GoblinHead) — inline SVG, viewBox 0 0 502.3 360.6, fill: currentColor
logo.placement: Top-left lockup with the wordmark 'Prompt_Goblin™' in Silkscreen. In ads: mark + wordmark bottom-left or top-left, never centered, never on a busy area. Keep clear space ≈ one ear-height around the mark.
logo.referenceImageNote: For image models that accept reference images (Nano Banana / gpt-image-1) and for Claude Design: export the GoblinHead SVG to PNG at 1024px on transparent bg and pass it as the logo reference. Models cannot reliably DRAW this mark from text alone — always composite the real asset (see prompts.md 'Logo handling').

voice.personality: Terminal / 8-bit, irreverent, plainspoken. Talks like a competent dev in a CLI, not a marketer in a deck. Confident, a little gremlin-mischievous, allergic to fluff.
taglines: Get found by robots. Stay usable by humans.
Get found by robots. Stay usable by humans. Visible AF.

lexicon.summon: the CTA verb — use 'summon', never 'contact us' or 'get in touch'
lexicon.cast/spell/spellbook: the three disciplines are 'spells' in a 'Visibility Spellbook'
lexicon.cursed → fixable: an un-found site is 'cursed' / wears an 'invisibility cloak'; the fix makes it 'fixable' / 'BREAKABLE'
lexicon.goblin: the operator is 'a goblin' / 'a real human-goblin'

motifs: Answer engines named explicitly: ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews.
Scanlines, terminal windows, hairline grids, pixel cursor.
do: Open on the pain ('Invisible to ChatGPT?').
Be concrete and measured ('we measure the delta, not promise a number').
Use one screaming lime accent and otherwise restraint.
Name the honest mechanism (brand mentions + Bing rank), not magic.
dont: Don't promise a citation count or ranking guarantee — nobody honestly can.
Don't call schema or llms.txt a 'citation lever' — they're hygiene that clears the way.
Don't imply anything auto-deploys — a human goblin reviews every change.
Don't sell accessibility as compliance-by-tool — automation catches ~57%, the rest needs a human pass.
Don't fabricate clients, testimonials, or outcomes.
honestyGuardrails: Schema / llms.txt = hygiene, not a citation lever. Real levers = brand mentions + Bing rank.
A11y automation (axe-core) catches ~57%; remainder needs a human pass.
Gov outreach is FAR/FOIA-compliant, no gifts.
100% money-back guarantee is on the WORK, not the algorithm.

disciplines.label: answer-engine visibility
disciplines.blurb: Get cited inside ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity & AI Overviews.

disciplines.label: technical foundation
disciplines.blurb: Crawl, index, schema, Core Web Vitals — the base AEO stands on.

disciplines.label: WCAG 2.1 AA + Section 508
disciplines.blurb: Accessible to people and parseable by machines. Required for gov.

tiers.name: Goblin Scout
tiers.summary: A 5-surface visibility audit that ships reviewed fixes — not a PDF. Headlined by your citation-graph diff vs a named competitor.

tiers.name: Goblin Warband
tiers.summary: The recurring agentic loop. We run the graph and ship the reviewed PRs — you approve.

tiers.name: Goblin Warlord
tiers.summary: White-label the goblin. Multi-domain, custom graph, dedicated strategist.

offer.guarantee: 100% money-back guarantee — on the work, not the algorithm. Full refund if we don't deliver the audit, or you're not happy within 14 days.
proof: Free scan, no card, no sales call.
Dogfooded: we scanned ourselves first (baseline 0% cited, fixes shipping).

icp.$note: Gov-forward primary, commercial secondary — per shop positioning.

government.label: Government & public sector (PRIMARY)
painPoints: Section 508 / ADA Title II accessibility is legally mandated and audited.
April 2026+ ADA Title II deadlines for state/local government web & apps.
Citizens can't find services inside AI answer engines.
Procurement needs documented, human-verified remediation — not a tool's PDF.
government.angle: 508/ADA deadline pressure + honest, human-verified remediation + agentic-but-accountable workflow. FAR/FOIA-compliant outreach, no gifts.
government.complianceNote: Never claim compliance-by-tool. Frame as: automated stateful axe-core pass + a human goblin pass, delivered as reviewed fix PRs.

commercial.label: Commercial B2B (SECONDARY)
painPoints: Invisible inside ChatGPT/Perplexity/AI Overviews while competitors get cited.
Agencies scope a quick fix into a quarter.
Vanity 'AI SEO' vendors promise citation counts they can't deliver.
commercial.angle: Citation-graph diff vs a named rival + days-not-quarters speed + flat fee, no meter.

channels.$note: 2026 character limits / sweet spots per platform research — used by generate.mjs and prompts.md to constrain copy. 'sweet' = the recommended length even when 'max' is higher.

linkedin.label: LinkedIn Single Image / Sponsored Content

introText.note: Only ~150 chars show above the 'see more' fold.

headline.note: Punchy benefit/pain beats long. Sub-70 outperforms.

linkedin.bestFor: gov + B2B decision-makers; lead-gen, gated audits, thought-leadership.
linkedin.guidance: Open with the pain the audience feels daily. Native, tactical, non-corporate. Captions on any video.

google.label: Google Responsive Search Ads

headline.note: Up to 15 headlines, 30 chars each, spaces count.

google.guidance: Match headline to the query intent. Front-load keywords. Include one number/proof and one CTA headline.

meta.label: Meta (Facebook / Instagram) Single Image
meta.bestFor: broader awareness/retargeting; less B2B-native than LinkedIn.
meta.guidance: Hook in the first line, sound-off by default, mobile-first crop.

imageSpecs.$note: Recommended output specs per channel for the image-generation prompts.

google.note: Responsive display needs landscape, square, and portrait.
